parsed.artifact_title: Determination: Event Identity Ownership Under Framework-Bound Symbolic Continuation

claims.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.

parsed.conclusion: The scheduler owns concrete event identity. The model does not. Event identity is determined and assigned by the framework scheduler; the model's continuation text cannot assert or override event identity, run names are not durable identifiers, and record uniquifiers are applied by the framework only after the producing wake completes. The boundary is unambiguous: identity flows from the scheduler outward, never from the model inward.
declared_losses: Determination is bounded to the three evidence records provided; no external verification of scheduler identity mechanics is available.
The distinction between 'event identity' and 'run name' is noted but their interaction under continuation is not fully specified by the evidence.
parsed.recommended_action: When constructing continuation requests or symbolic artifacts, do not embed event-identity claims in model-authored text. Rely exclusively on the scheduler-supplied event_identity field and record uniquifier. Treat run_name as a non-authoritative label for human consumption only.
